title: icon deleted from task bar
original_question: I accidently deleted an icon for microsoft solitaire from my task bar.   Is there a way to get it back?
preliminary_solution: You can restore the icon by searching for "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" in the Start Menu, right-clicking the app, and selecting "Pin to taskbar."
final_solution: You can restore the icon by searching for "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" in the Start Menu, right-clicking the app, and selecting "Pin to taskbar."
accepted_answer: Hi,  This covers how to get a Desktop Shortcut and get your Taskbar icon back:     Left click on far left Icon in the Taskbar > then in the "Start" Window click on down Arrow at bottom left to go to "Apps by Category" Window > locate the App > right click on it and select "Open file location" > in the next Window that presents itself you right click on App from the list > run Mouse Cursor over "Send to" > select "Desktop (create shortcut)".  And while you are at the "Apps by Category" Window with it > right click on the particular App > select "Pin to Taskbar"   Cheers.

title: How can I restore the old version of the calculator on Windows 8.1?
original_question: Original title: The old version of calculator I have recently upgraded from 8.0 to 8.1.  There is a new version of the calculator that I don't particularly like.  I would like to restore the old version of the calculator if I can if you help me.  Thanks.
preliminary_solution: The classic desktop calculator (calc.exe) is still available in Windows 8.1; you can usually find it by searching for "calculator" in the Start screen and selecting the desktop app, or by navigating to C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe.
final_solution: The classic desktop calculator (calc.exe) is still available in Windows 8.1; you can usually find it by searching for "calculator" in the Start screen and selecting the desktop app, or by navigating to C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe.
accepted_answer:
Hi,  Welcome to Microsoft community forum. There are two types of calculator on Windows 8.1, a modern one and a desktop one. You cannot restore the older version of calculator on Windows 8.1. For more information on Calculator app refer this Microsoft article:  Calculator app for Windows: FLarryQ http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-8/calculator-app-faq    Hope this helps. If you need further assistance with Windows, let us know and will be glad to help.

Hi,   Thanks for the update. There is no need to search for calculator each time from start screen. You can try these steps to easily access calculator. title: Edit Comment in Excel 2007
original_question: When editing comments in Microsoft Excel 2007 by right clicking and choosing “Edit Comments” that is usually the only task required. Something happened within the last week and now when right clicking and choosing “Edit Comments” it is unresponsive to the request, unless now choosing “Show/Hide Comment” first … then going back to choose “Edit Comments”. Why is this extra step now required, when usually it is not required and what changes needs to be made to so that “Show/Hide Comment” is no longer necessary like usual?
preliminary_solution: Check Excel Options, specifically under the 'Advanced' section, for any settings related to comments or object interaction that might have changed, or try disabling any recently installed Excel add-ins.
cq_1: Does this issue occur in all Excel workbooks, including newly created ones, or only in specific existing files?
solution_1: Try opening Excel in Safe Mode (hold Ctrl while launching Excel) to determine if the problem is caused by add-ins or startup files. If the issue is resolved in Safe Mode, investigate disabling add-ins or repairing your Excel installation.
cq_2: Have you tried opening Excel in Safe Mode to see if the issue persists?
solution_2: Since diagnostic information is unavailable, try checking Excel Options under 'Advanced' for comment display settings, then manually disable any recently installed add-ins via 'Excel Options' > 'Add-Ins'. If the issue persists, consider repairing your Microsoft Office 2007 installation.
final_solution: Since diagnostic information is unavailable, try checking Excel Options under 'Advanced' for comment display settings, then manually disable any recently installed add-ins via 'Excel Options' > 'Add-Ins'. If the issue persists, consider repairing your Microsoft Office 2007 installation.
accepted_answer: Hi,   I have heard of this problem before but never seen it.   As a sensible precaution make a backup of you workbook.   Ensure the problem worksheet is the active (visible) sheet and try this VB code.   ALT+F11 to open vb editor. Right click 'ThisWorkbook' and insert module and paste the code below in on the right.   While still in VB editor, tap the F5 key to run the code, it should run very quickly so don't be surprised if nothing seems to have happened. Go back to the worksheet and test your comments.     Sub RepairComments() Dim CmntRange As Range Dim C As Range Dim Cmnt As String On Error Resume Next     Set CmntRange = ActiveSheet.Cells.SpecialCells(xlCellTypeComments)     If CmntRange Is Nothing Then         MsgBox "no comments on sheet"         Exit Sub     End If  For Each C In CmntRange     Cmnt = C.Comment.Text     C.Comment.Delete     C.AddComment     C.Comment.Text Text:=Cmnt Next End Sub

preliminary_solution: Since sdclt.exe /configure works directly, the issue is likely specific to your user profile or a corrupted shell association. Try creating a new local user account and see if "Back up your computer" works from the Control Panel in that new account.
final_solution: Since sdclt.exe /configure works directly, the issue is likely specific to your user profile or a corrupted shell association. Try creating a new local user account and see if "Back up your computer" works from the Control Panel in that new account.
accepted_answer: Hi,   Since how long have you had this issue?   I’d suggest you to open event viewer  and check if there are any significant error messages/codes related to the issue. title: Setting will open but I cannot see them. Windows 10
original_question: For around a month my settings would disappear whenever I opened. It will still show on the task bar and still show the tab when I hover my mouse over it but when I clicked it it did nothing. I didn't want to reinstall steam because I would lose all of my data on my external hard drive. I bring this to your attention now because steam started doing the same exact thing. So now I can no longer play games I didn't create shortcuts too. If somebody knows how you can fix this, please tell me.
preliminary_solution: Try selecting the problematic application on the taskbar, then press Alt + Spacebar, followed by M, and then use your arrow keys to move the window back onto the screen, pressing Enter when visible.
cq_1: Does this issue affect other applications besides Settings and Steam, and are you using multiple monitors or have you recently changed your display setup?
user_response_1: The issue also affects the Microsoft Store app, but information about multiple monitors or display setup changes is not available.
solution_1: Since multiple applications are affected, first try selecting the problematic app on the taskbar, then press Alt + Spacebar, followed by M, and use arrow keys to move the window back onto the screen. If this doesn't work, update your display drivers and consider creating a new user profile to check if the issue persists there.
final_solution: Since multiple applications are affected, first try selecting the problematic app on the taskbar, then press Alt + Spacebar, followed by M, and use arrow keys to move the window back onto the screen. If this doesn't work, update your display drivers and consider creating a new user profile to check if the issue persists there.
accepted_answer: show on the task bar and still show the tab when I hover my mouse over it but when I clicked it it did nothing.   Check to see if you have a window that is moved off screen (or located on another monitor).  Right-click the Taskbar icon's thumbnail when you have raised it and choose Move from the thumbnail's window menu.  Notice the change in mouse pointer but in your case you can not use it to drag.  What you can do however is use a cursor key to engage the mouse pointer on the window's title bar for a drag move.  Then drag move the window into view.  In the case where the window is on a different monitor you would also have the (simpler) alternative of using a  Win+Shift+Cursor  move.   Good luck Glenn Aldwinckle ---

title: VLOOKUP for time stamp sometimes returns incorrect value?
original_question: I have a table with a column of time stamps. These come from an external source and the cell is configured as "custom h:mm". I have created a 2nd table with deduplicated values of the time stamps. Then I am using a VLOOKUP function to match the values from the deduplicated table to the original one. Some of the return values are correct, while others are incorrect, and I don't understand why. I've tried to use VALUE function and change the columns to another form of data other than time, but it did not work. This is an example from the original table. Column A is the time stamps and column B is the return values from the second table (below).   0:11 11 1:06 18 1:09 21 1:11 23 2:06 15 2:05 29 1:03 11 2:10 34 1:02 11 2:06 15 2:06 15 6:06 78 3:05 14 This is the second table I have created: 0:11 11 1:06 18 1:09 21 1:11 23 2:06 30 2:05 29 1:03 15 2:10 34 1:02 14 6:06 78 3:05 41 As you can see, values like 0:11, 2:05 and 6:06 return correctly, while values for 2:06 and 3:05 do not. I have no idea why this is happening. Any ideas? P.S. The source of the original time stamp comes from a very old external software and there is no way to reimport the data differently.
preliminary_solution: Ensure your VLOOKUP function uses FALSE as the fourth argument for an exact match. If the issue persists, consider rounding both sets of time stamps to the nearest minute using a function like MROUND to eliminate potential precision differences from the external source.
cq_1: Are you using FALSE as the fourth argument in your VLOOKUP function to ensure an exact match?
solution_1: Ensure your VLOOKUP function explicitly uses FALSE (or 0) as the fourth argument for an exact match. If the issue persists, apply the MROUND function to both sets of time stamps, rounding to the nearest minute, to mitigate potential floating-point precision differences.
final_solution: Ensure your VLOOKUP function explicitly uses FALSE (or 0) as the fourth argument for an exact match. If the issue persists, apply the MROUND function to both sets of time stamps, rounding to the nearest minute, to mitigate potential floating-point precision differences.
accepted_answer: Hi Hayden,   Have you viewed joeu2004Brittany's reply? I would like to explain that when we use VLOOKUP, by default, the table must be sorted in an ascending order. @ joeu2004Brittany, @AshishTroy Mathur, thanks for your suggestions.   @nimrodyanaiHayden, in your case, I would like to suggest you use Advance Filter. We don't need to sort the data and delete duplicate data manually.   Here are the detailed steps: 1.Create two sheets, named Original and Deduplicated. The original data is copied from the table you provided.  2.Navigate to Deduplicated, click Data>Advanced. The Advanced Filter dialogue box will appear. Select Copy to another location.    3.Click List range>Original, choose the list range. Click Copy to, choose where you want to place the filtered data. Select Unique records only.    4.Click OK, the filtered data will appear.    Regards, ZixuanDominic

title: Changing font styles used in the Navigation Pane or comment Balloons in Word 2016
original_question: I recently contacted the help desk for Office 2016 for the Mac about changing the font sizes in the Navigation Pane and in the Comment balloons. I could not figure out a way to do this from within the Navigation Pane or the Review menu.  I tried several levels of Microsoft Support but we couldn’t make it work. After hanging up, I kept trying for a while and here’s is what finally worked:  Navigation Pane:  Have the Navigation Pane open. Select Format/Styles from the Word menu. Choose “Document Map” style under the Styles pull-down menu. Choose the styles/fonts/sizes that you want. Click Apply.  Comment Balloons:  Have comment balloons displayed and then highlight some text from any comment. Select Format/Styles from the Word menu. Choose the ‘Comment Text’ style. Make your changes and then click Apply. This is new to me but it seems to work every time. I would appreciate seeing if others can validate this approach.
preliminary_solution: Your described method for changing font styles in the Navigation Pane (using 'Document Map' style) and Comment Balloons (using 'Comment Text' style) via Format/Styles is indeed the correct and standard approach in Word for Mac. This is how Word's style system is designed to manage these elements.
final_solution: Your described method for changing font styles in the Navigation Pane (using 'Document Map' style) and Comment Balloons (using 'Comment Text' style) via Format/Styles is indeed the correct and standard approach in Word for Mac. This is how Word's style system is designed to manage these elements.
accepted_answer: As I wrote, the Document Map style controlled the appearance of entries in the Document Map in older versions of Word. The appearance of entries in the Navigation pane can't be customized at all. (The Document Map style exists only for backward compatibility reasons.)  That is how it works in Windows versions of Word. We will have to wait for someone to confirm how it works in Word 2016 for Mac.

title: Highlight the answer in a column
original_question: I have a table of data. It is several hundred rows. I show the min, max and mean of a particular column. Is there a way, in addition to getting the answer, to highlight (different color or background) where it is in the column? It would save time trying to find it. Thanks.
preliminary_solution: You can achieve this using Conditional Formatting with formulas. For each value (min, max, mean), select the column, go to Conditional Formatting > New Rule > Use a formula to determine which cells to format, and enter a formula like "=A1=MIN($A:$A)" (adjust A1 and $A:$A to your column) for each condition, applying a different format.
final_solution: You can achieve this using Conditional Formatting with formulas. For each value (min, max, mean), select the column, go to Conditional Formatting > New Rule > Use a formula to determine which cells to format, and enter a formula like "=A1=MIN($A:$A)" (adjust A1 and $A:$A to your column) for each condition, applying a different format.
accepted_answer: I think Aaron means, in the cells in the column with numbers, add three conditional formatting rules to each cell. You can create the rules in one cell and then copy...paste special...formats to the other cells in the column.   One rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MIN of the column and applies the color; the second rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MAX of the column and applies that color; the third does the same for the average.  So only two to three cells would be colored in the column of numbers.

final_solution: Since the specific coordinate system and API usage are unknown, the most likely issue is that your 'X and Y' coordinates are not in the WGS84 Longitude/Latitude format expected by Bing Maps, or they are being passed in the wrong order (e.g., X as Latitude, Y as Longitude). SuStacyFirstNonEmptyCell()            Dim startCell As Range, firstNonEmptyCell As Range       Set startCell = Range("AJ:AJ")       If VBA.IsEmpty(startCell.Value) Then         MsgBox "No data in this column"     Else         Set firstNonEmptyCell = startCell.End(xlDown)                   Range("AM1") = firstNonEmptyCell.Value >>> Instead would like it to be first value in Range AJ that is not NO             End If         Call CopyDown                End Sub        A                  B                    C               D          E                F                 AH              AI            AJ                 AK             AL       AM 353514199 L17     F     F     7777788899 NO FEL1 353514102 L17     N     F     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112222 FEL1 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514199 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     3333332222 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     3333333222 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     3333332222 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     3333222222 NO 353514C99 L17     E     N     8675309651 FEL1 353514Z99 L17     E     F     7727272727 NO
preliminary_solution: You'll need to modify your VBA code to loop through each cell in column AJ, starting from the top, and assign the value of the first cell that is not 'NO' to AM1. If no such cell is found after checking the entire column, AM1 should display 'No Matches Found in Search.'

title: Bullets in the Table for PowerPoint
original_question: Hi, I am struggling to figure out why when I press the bullet button to format the texts in the table, the bullet only formats the first line break. For example, if I copy an excel text from a cell with line breaks like this: Apple Oranges Cabbage and paste it into a PowerPoint table cell and press bullet, only the first bullet will have a bullet while the others have hanging indents like this •      Apple Oranges Cabbages I would like the text within the table cell to look like this •       Apple •       Oranges •       Cabbages Currently, I have to manually create the bullets by erasing the pre-made line breaks from excel and pressing enter. However, this can get tedious. Is there a way to fix this problem? Thank you
preliminary_solution: The issue occurs because PowerPoint treats line breaks (soft returns) differently from paragraph breaks. To fix this, after pasting the text into the table cell, select the text, then use Find and Replace (Ctrl+H) to replace "^l" (manual line break) with "^p" (paragraph break) before applying bullets.
final_solution: The issue occurs because PowerPoint treats line breaks (soft returns) differently from paragraph breaks. To fix this, after pasting the text into the table cell, select the text, then use Find and Replace (Ctrl+H) to replace "^l" (manual line break) with "^p" (paragraph break) before applying bullets.
accepted_answer: Hi Deborah Park,  Based on your description, when you click the Bullet option, only the first text are formatted with the bullet. I would like to suggest you ensure you have selected the whole text, and select the bullet in PowerPoint. You can find more details in this article: Add bullets or numbers to text  If the issue persists, could you please provide detailed steps and related screenshots about how you copy an excel text from a cell with line breaks and encounter the issue, so we can better understand your situation and help you?  Regards, YogaStephanie

title: export to CD option in ppt2016
original_question: The embedded music doesn't play on powerpoint 2016 when I exported it to a memory stick-  I used mp3's '
preliminary_solution: First, verify that the MP3 files are actually present in the folder created by the 'Package Presentation for CD' feature on your memory stick. If they are missing, manually copy them into that folder alongside your presentation file.
cq_1: When you inserted the MP3s into your PowerPoint presentation, did you choose to 'Embed' them directly or 'Link' to the files?
user_response_1: The presentation was created with embedded background music.
solution_1: Since the music is embedded, the 'Package Presentation for CD' feature should have included the MP3 files; if they are not playing, first verify their presence in the packaged folder, and if found, investigate playback issues on the target computer.
cq_2: Given that the music is embedded, when you open the folder created by the 'Package Presentation for CD' feature on your memory stick, do you see the MP3 files present within that folder or any subfolders (like 'MEDIA')?
solution_2: Since the music is embedded and you cannot verify the presence of the MP3 files in the packaged folder, test the exported presentation on the original computer from the memory stick to determine if the issue is with the export process or the playback environment on other machines.
cq_3: When you attempt to play the presentation from the memory stick, does the music fail to play on *all* computers you've tried, or only on specific ones?
final_solution: To resolve the embedded music issue, first, re-package your presentation using "File > Export > Package Presentation for CD." After packaging, open the created folder on your memory stick and confirm the MP3 files are present, often in a 'MEDIA' subfolder. Then, test the presentation directly from the memory stick on your original computer. If the music still doesn't play, try re-embedding the MP3s, ensuring they are set to 'Play Across Slides' and 'Loop until Stopped' in PowerPoint's Playback options.
accepted_answer: Try this:  Delete the existing music files Put the music files in the same folder as the PowerPoint file Re-insert them  That generally produces "pathless" links that work on other systems so long as:  - The PPT/x and music files stay together in the same folder and - The computer is capable of playing the music files in the first place  But something else to check on ... see, it's odd that PPT should be complaining about linked files. The default nowadays is for it to embed music and video files, not link them, unless you explicitly told it to link (in which case, re-insert them, DON'T link and you should be ok).  Unless perhaps you're playing the presentation on an older version of PowerPoint; it might not be able to play embedded MP3s.  Something else to check ...

preliminary_solution: Access the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) by force-restarting your laptop multiple times during boot, then try booting into Safe Mode or using System Restore to revert to a previous working state.
cq_1: When you see your name and picture on the login screen, are there any other login options visible, such as a 'Sign-in options' link or icon?
solution_1: Since the login box is missing and no other sign-in options are visible, access the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) by force-restarting your laptop multiple times during boot, then navigate to Troubleshoot > Advanced options to try System Restore or boot into Safe Mode to diagnose the issue.
final_solution: Since the login box is missing and no other sign-in options are visible, access the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) by force-restarting your laptop multiple times during boot, then navigate to Troubleshoot > Advanced options to try System Restore or boot into Safe Mode to diagnose the issue.
accepted_answer: Is there perhaps a left-pointing arrow near your account tile on the login screen?  You can try that to access alternate accounts.  If that doesn't work, let's try to restore back to the last day it was working.  At the bottom of the login screen, you'll see a power icon.  Hold the shift key on your keyboard while you choose the Restart option.  After a few seconds, some options will appear.  Here you can use troubleshooting and then System Restore.  This restores backups of your registry and system settings, but doesn’t touch any of your files. It only takes a couple of minutes. title: Free remote desktop to control windows 7 PC with my android phone?
original_question: Hey I was wondering if their is a free remote desktop program for my windows 7 PC, so I can access my computer from my android phone? I don't want to  control my computer, just access it from a different server. Example, if I am on the app on my phone I want to be able to use my computer at the same time separately. Most apps only act as a remote to navigate/ control you computer and you can watch the mouse move on screen, which is not what I want. If anyone can help please I could use it. Also if you are confused, let me know and I will try to reword it or explain it better. Please and thanks in advance.
preliminary_solution: Standard remote desktop applications for Windows 7 typically mirror or take over the main desktop session. If you're looking to access files, stream media, or run specific background tasks without a visual desktop takeover, consider setting up a dedicated file sharing or media server application on your PC, paired with a compatible app on your Android phone.
cq_1: Could you describe what specific actions or tasks you intend to perform on your Windows 7 PC from your Android phone, given that you don't want to control the main desktop or see the mouse move?
solution_1: Your request for simultaneous, separate access to a Windows 7 PC from an Android phone, without controlling the main desktop, is not natively supported by Windows 7. Client versions of Windows are designed for a single interactive user session at a time; achieving concurrent, independent sessions typically requires a Windows Server operating system with Remote Desktop Services, or specific third-party solutions that may have limitations or licensing implications.
final_solution: Your request for simultaneous, separate access to a Windows 7 PC from an Android phone, without controlling the main desktop, is not natively supported by Windows 7. Client versions of Windows are designed for a single interactive user session at a time; achieving concurrent, independent sessions typically requires a Windows Server operating system with Remote Desktop Services, or specific third-party solutions that may have limitations or licensing implications.
accepted_answer: You need a server operating system then.  http://www.microsoft.com/windowsserver2008/en/us/rds-product-home.aspx Likely the company uses a combination of roaming profiles and RDS so you are actually logging into a Virtual Machine.

title: I have a data sheet that I need to Fill out - Excel 2010
original_question: I have a data sheet that I will be filling out often. I am trying to set up the formulas so that when the title or pc of equipment in entered from a drop down list, 3 other cells will automatically populate. Sheet 1 A          |       B        |             C              |          D      | Lift #    |  Serial #    |   Manufacturer    |  Model #   | When I enter info for Column A (116 possibilities currently) from a drop down menu, I want B, C, and D to populate Sheet 2 A          |       B        |             C              |          D      | Lift #    |  Serial #    |   Manufacturer    |  Model #   | --------------------------------------------------------- Lift 1   | 32323232 |  Sunset Blvd         |  32SBGT    | ....and you can multiply this type of information by 116, but the list does grow every year as some equipment is added. I have tried to use Vlookup, If Value, HLookup. I am clearly missing something. Is there a single formula, a multiple formula? Something where this will continue down the entire workbook as at the end of each year, I could literally have well over 10,000 lines and I do need this information to populate. I am not going to be the only one using this document, and I do not want errors from people typing in and misreading documentation Thank you!!
preliminary_solution: Set up VLOOKUP formulas in Sheet 1 for columns B, C, and D, referencing the selected 'Lift #' in column A and looking up the corresponding data from Sheet 2, ensuring you use FALSE for an exact match and absolute references for your lookup table range.
final_solution: Set up VLOOKUP formulas in Sheet 1 for columns B, C, and D, referencing the selected 'Lift #' in column A and looking up the corresponding data from Sheet 2, ensuring you use FALSE for an exact match and absolute references for your lookup table range.
accepted_answer: This VLOOKUP should work then (assuming Sheet 2 is actually called "Sheet 2" with a space before the 2. In cell B2, enter this:  =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,2,FALSE),"")  In C2:   =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,3,FALSE),"")  And in D2:  =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,3,FALSE),"")  The IFERROR function shows a blank in each cell if nothing is picked in the dropdowns in column A.  You'll just need to copy the formulas down the columns.

title: Linking cells in a table across multiple worksheets
original_question: So I have an excel document that has 100+ worksheets in it. We use this to list all of the various materials that we need to purchase for a project. For example, if we are using 5 different sizes of screws, we would list all of these out and then have a total quantity needed cell beside it.  Most of these sheets are identically set up in the same manner, with the exception that the name of the sheet is different and the various quantities will vary on each sheet. On our main page (Master BOM), we type out all of the different items and then we go onto the various worksheets (UNIT NAMES) that use those products. On these pages we type the quantity we need and we have it set up to where all of these sheet quantities are totaled on the Master BOM sheet. What I am now trying to do is create another sheet that lists the names of all of the worksheets and then beside these names list the total number of units being made. For example, if we are building 3 different sized boxes named Box A, Box B, and Box C, and we are ordering 2,4, & 6, respectively, I have a cell labeled Box A and the cell beside it has a quantity of 2 in it. Make sense? So I have my list completely done and the quantities listed beside them. What I am needing to do is have that corresponding quantity automatically link to the worksheet for that particular unit. So using my example above, I have Box A listed with 2 quantities. I want that 2 (or whatever number I have to update it to) to automatically link to the worksheet that I have for Box A (and yes the worksheet is labeled as Box A already). Is there a way to do this without me having to go through every single sheet and type that it is equal to a certain cell? I do have Kutools if that helps.
preliminary_solution: You can achieve this by placing a single formula on each unit worksheet that dynamically looks up its own sheet name on your 'Unit Totals' sheet. On each individual unit worksheet, in the cell where you want the total quantity to appear, enter a formula like: =INDEX('Unit Totals'!B:B, MATCH(MID(CELL("filename",A1),FIND("]",CELL("filename",A1))+1,255),'Unit Totals'!A:A,0)) (adjust 'Unit Totals' to your actual sheet name, and B:B/A:A to your actual columns for quantities and unit names, respectively). Then, copy this formula to the same target cell on all other unit worksheets.
final_solution: You can achieve this by placing a single formula on each unit worksheet that dynamically looks up its own sheet name on your 'Unit Totals' sheet. On each individual unit worksheet, in the cell where you want the total quantity to appear, enter a formula like: =INDEX('Unit Totals'!B:B, MATCH(MID(CELL("filename",A1),FIND("]",CELL("filename",A1))+1,255),'Unit Totals'!A:A,0)) (adjust 'Unit Totals' to your actual sheet name, and B:B/A:A to your actual columns for quantities and unit names, respectively). Then, copy this formula to the same target cell on all other unit worksheets.
accepted_answer: That's great The snip shows the formula to use Note depending on what is in Takeoff you will need to adjust the range: =VLOOKUP(D2,Takeoff!B4:C20,2,FALSE)  With lots of worksheets it would be a chore do enter a formula in everyone. The trick is to group the worksheets first: 1) click on the BoxA tab 2)Hold down SHIFT and click on thtab of the last worksheet needing the formula; the tabs will now all be bright. 3)Type the formula; each worksheet will get a copy 4) Right click the tab of BoxA and select Ungroup   Hope this gets you on the right road

title: Convert picture to drawing
original_question: I have a jpg of a caricature drawing, and I would like to convert it back to a drawing.  I remember doing this some time ago (probably an earlier version of PowerPoint), but I can't seem to remember how in Office 2010.  I thought it was "Edit Drawing" "Edit points", or something, but I can't find the command now.  Can anyone remind me how this is done in Office 2010?  I also have 2013 if necessary. Thanks,
preliminary_solution: PowerPoint 2010/2013 does not have a built-in feature to automatically convert a raster JPG image into editable vector shapes. The 'Edit Drawing' and 'Edit Points' options you recall are typically for vector graphics (like WMF/EMF files) or grouped shapes created within PowerPoint.
cq_1: When you say 'convert it back to a drawing,' are you looking for a way to automatically vectorize the JPG into editable shapes within PowerPoint, or are you open to manually tracing it?
solution_1: PowerPoint 2010/2013 does not have a built-in feature to automatically convert a JPG into editable vector shapes. The 'Edit Points' functionality you recall applies to shapes created directly in PowerPoint or to ungrouped vector images (like WMF/EMF files). To achieve a 'drawing' effect from your JPG, you would need to manually trace it using PowerPoint's Freeform or Curve shape tools, or use an external vectorization program.
final_solution: PowerPoint 2010/2013 does not have a built-in feature to automatically convert a JPG into editable vector shapes. The 'Edit Points' functionality you recall applies to shapes created directly in PowerPoint or to ungrouped vector images (like WMF/EMF files). To achieve a 'drawing' effect from your JPG, you would need to manually trace it using PowerPoint's Freeform or Curve shape tools, or use an external vectorization program.
accepted_answer: PowerPoint's never been able to convert raster images (like your JPG) to vector.   Raster = a collection of variously colored dots that happen to look like a picture Vector = mathematically defined lines/text/shapes that make up a picture  PowerPoint and many other programs can convert vector graphics to raster but it takes a tracing program to go the other way.  PowerPoint doesn't do it ... sorry.

title: I deleted Temp Folder and StartUp Menu on accident now some apps are missing...
original_question: Ok,so here is the problem, which honestly I've had for months now...I was trying to cleanup some files on my computer,well i read somewhere that you don't need your Temp folder so i deleted it i also read something about StartMenu folder taking up a lot of space......so i deleted it, it ask for Administrator permission so I clicked ok and it deleted the files, not only that but i deleted it from my recycle bin. really important things such as Desktop and Shop were gone! I was able to get Desktop Back from another forum post that had a download which currently now is fine, but that not the worst part, my Change PC setting's is gone as well,when i click it nothing happens and i mean nothing if i also try changing my account picture on the metro screen as well nothing happens just....nothing. If anyone has a solution please post it, also another thing that has been happening rapidly. Every time i turn off my PC the next day i turn it on my WiFi is disconnected,if it's not then in the middle of the day it will say *WIFI NAME* Limited and i get disconnected, what does it mean and how do i fix it, I've run Virus scans with Norton and nothing has come up well something but i fixed it,anyways PLEASE HELP!!!
preliminary_solution: Given the deletion of critical system folders, the most effective solution is to perform a System Refresh or Reset to restore core Windows files. For the Wi-Fi issue, try reinstalling your network adapter drivers.
cq_1: Do you have any system restore points created before you deleted these folders, or access to Windows 8.1 installation media?
user_response_1: The only available system restore points are from months after the deletion, and there is no information about access to installation media.
solution_1: Since useful system restore points are unavailable, the most effective solution for the missing apps and PC settings is to perform a System Refresh or Reset, which will require Windows 8.1 installation media or a recovery partition. For the Wi-Fi issue, reinstall your network adapter drivers.
cq_2: Do you have access to the original Windows 8.1 installation media (USB or DVD) or a recovery partition on your computer?
solution_2: Without installation media or useful restore points, first try running 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' from an elevated command prompt to repair system files. If the missing apps and settings persist, create a new administrator user profile to see if the issue is profile-specific. For the Wi-Fi problem, reinstall your network adapter drivers.
final_solution: Without installation media or useful restore points, first try running 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' from an elevated command prompt to repair system files. If the missing apps and settings persist, create a new administrator user profile to see if the issue is profile-specific. For the Wi-Fi problem, reinstall your network adapter drivers.
accepted_answer: Some files can be deleted from the Temp folder, but not the folder itself because Windows uses it to hold temporary files in.  It's a bit like your car and it's gas tank. You can drain it, but you still have a fully functional automobile. But if you remove the gas tank, then you're not going to be going anywhere until you replace it.  You now find yourself in that situation with your computer. Until you replace the Temp folder and its contents, your machine is not going to function properly.  I think you're looking at having to Refresh the system in order to get it going again. What you'll have to do first is to create a Recovery disk and copy the manufacturer's partition to that. This means you'll have to go buy an external hard drive with at least 60GB free. But bearing in mind that the cost of a 60GB hard disk and one with a 500GB capacity is barely a matter of cents, you'll be better off with the large capacity drive which you can use for other purposes later.  To create the Recovery drive, hit Windows key to open the Start screen with the tiles and start typing the word "Recovery". You'll see the option called "Create a recovery drive" appear on the right. Click that and then click Next. Here's a screenshot.      Then follow instructions in this article: http://windows.microsoft.com/en-gb/windows-8/restore-refresh-reset-pc

title: Worksheet borders
original_question: This relates to Excel 2016 and pdf pages. For some unknown reason, I cannot go to the edge of my worksheet and find the arrow symbol that allows me to drag the border away from the edge of the page. I am trying to reduce the size of my view so I can view more than one program image at the same time. Never had this problem before in any Microsoft program.
preliminary_solution: If the Excel window is maximized, click the 'Restore Down' icon (the square with two overlapping squares) in the top-right corner of the window to enable resizing.
cq_1: Is the Excel application window currently maximized (filling your entire screen)?
user_response_1: The Excel worksheets currently fill the entire screen.
solution_1: Since Excel is maximized, click the 'Restore Down' icon (the square with two overlapping squares) in the top-right corner of the Excel window to un-maximize it, which will then allow you to drag the window borders to resize it.
final_solution: Since Excel is maximized, click the 'Restore Down' icon (the square with two overlapping squares) in the top-right corner of the Excel window to un-maximize it, which will then allow you to drag the window borders to resize it.
accepted_answer: You can only resize a window that is not maximized.  In the top right corner of every window, regardless of what application it is, you see icons to minimize, maximize/restore and the X for exit. When a window is maximized, it cannot be resized. You need to click the maximize/restore icon to restore the window. It is a toggle switch. The first screenshot shows what it looks like in a maximized window that takes up the whole screen. The second screenshot shows what it looks like if the window is not maximized. This window's borders can be dragged.     Another quick way in Windows 7 and higher to see windows side by side is to select one window, hold down the Window key on your keyboard and hit the right arrow. That will send the window to the right hand side of the screen and it will take up half of the monitor. In Windows 10 you now can click any of the other open windows and it will be arranged on the left hand side of the screen. In older Windows versions you need to repeat the step to select a window and use the Windows key with the left arrow.

title: Date format changes when doing a mail merge
original_question: I am using Word & Excel 2007 and am currently trying to do a mail merge.  I have a DOB field in excel and the date format throughout the excel spreadsheets are dd/mm/yy.  When i do the mail merge the DOB date appears as m/dd/yy. Any suggestions as how to change it to the dd/mm/yy format.   Thanks Keiley
preliminary_solution: To ensure the date format is correct, right-click the DOB merge field in Word, select "Toggle Field Codes," and add a date-time picture switch like \@ "dd/MM/yy" to the field code (e.g., { MERGEFIELD "DOB" \@ "dd/MM/yy" }).
final_solution: To ensure the date format is correct, right-click the DOB merge field in Word, select "Toggle Field Codes," and add a date-time picture switch like \@ "dd/MM/yy" to the field code (e.g., { MERGEFIELD "DOB" \@ "dd/MM/yy" }).
accepted_answer: Right-click the merge field. Select Toggle Field Codes from the popup menu. You'll see something like { MERGEFIELD "DOB" * MERGEFORMAT } Change the latter part so that it looks like { MERGEFIELD "DOB" @ "dd/MM/yyyy" } Press F9 to hide the field code and to update the result.

title: if POWERPNT.EXE is already running on the machine when the application attempts to create a Power Point Application object that the Application contstructor throws an exception
original_question: System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException (0x80080005): Retrieving the COM class factory for component with CLSID {91493441-5A91-11CF-8700-00AA0060263B} failed due to the following error: 80080005.
preliminary_solution: This error often indicates a DCOM permission issue; try adjusting the DCOM security settings for Microsoft PowerPoint to grant 'Launch and Activation Permissions' to the user account running your application.
cq_1: Is the application attempting to create the PowerPoint object running as a service, a scheduled task, or under a different user account than the interactive desktop user?
solution_1: The 0x80080005 error typically indicates a DCOM server execution failure. Ensure the user account running your application has 'Launch and Activation Permissions' for Microsoft PowerPoint in DCOM security settings. Also, be aware that Office applications are designed for interactive desktop use and are generally not supported for server-side or unattended automation, which can lead to such errors if an instance is already running or the session is non-interactive.
final_solution: The 0x80080005 error typically indicates a DCOM server execution failure. Ensure the user account running your application has 'Launch and Activation Permissions' for Microsoft PowerPoint in DCOM security settings. Also, be aware that Office applications are designed for interactive desktop use and are generally not supported for server-side or unattended automation, which can lead to such errors if an instance is already running or the session is non-interactive.

title: Object Duplicate vs. Copy & Paste - who to put the money on?
original_question: I'm trying to piece together the difference between Copy+Paste and Duplicate with respect to a Slide but more so objects. I've seen an article or two, but they mix and match duplicating in Powerpoint. Is there like a MVP written blog on the real deal. I know one is it doesn't flush the most recent Copy and Paste buffer. I'm left disappointed by blog postings such as this: http://iconlogic.blogs.com/weblog/2011/06/powerpoint-2010-whats-the-difference-between-duplicate-and-copy.html
preliminary_solution: Duplicate (Ctrl+D) creates a copy of the selected object(s) or slide(s) and places it with a slight offset, without using the clipboard. Copy (Ctrl+C) and Paste (Ctrl+V) uses the clipboard, allowing you to paste the item multiple times or into other applications, and its placement can vary based on previous pastes or default settings.
final_solution: Duplicate (Ctrl+D) creates a copy of the selected object(s) or slide(s) and places it with a slight offset, without using the clipboard. Copy (Ctrl+C) and Paste (Ctrl+V) uses the clipboard, allowing you to paste the item multiple times or into other applications, and its placement can vary based on previous pastes or default settings.

I personally prefer to use Duplicate depending on the task when working on a specific slide. Also, you can Duplicate a selection by holding Option or Control while dragging it. title: Skype Thinks I Need Parental Consent
original_question: I have been using Skype on my iPhone for many years without issue.  I also use it on my desktop.  This morning it asked me to sign in again (I suspect it updated) so I did and then it asked for my birthday.  I entered the year incorrectly.  Now Skype wants me to get a parent to allow me to use it (even though I'm using it on my desktop right now and have been using it on my phone for several years).  I went to the site it suggested account.microsoft.com and tried to add myself as a family member, but it says I already am a family member and there are no others. I updated my birthday in the Profile and that worked.  I tried deleting the app from my phone and even powering down for a few minutes then reloading and the same thing happens. I tried chatting with Microsoft support and they said, "not my problem" and directed me to a link that doesn't work. I tried chatting with Skype support and they said that 10am PST time is not part of their working hours. So, I'm trying here.  I've wasted about two hours trying to get this to work again.  Is there any way to fix it without losing my account.  I'm afraid to log off on my desktop in case it won't let me back there either. BTW, the same app lets me log in with my MSFT email and password, but then, of course, all of my contacts and conversations are gone. Thanks, Tiffany Serface
preliminary_solution: Since you've updated your birthday and reinstalled the app, try clearing Safari's website data (Settings > Safari > Clear History and Website Data) on your iPhone, as Skype often uses web views for authentication, and then attempt to sign in again.
cq_1: Have you checked your Microsoft Family Safety settings on account.microsoft.com/family to ensure your account isn't listed as a child, even if you're the only member?
user_response_1: I went to account.microsoft.com and tried to add myself as a family member, but the site stated I already am a family member and there are no others.
solution_1: Since you've confirmed you're not listed as a child in a family group, the issue likely stems from a persistent age flag on your Microsoft account or an incomplete age verification. Ensure your Microsoft account's age is fully verified as an adult, and if you haven't already, try clearing Safari's website data (Settings > Safari > Clear History and Website Data) on your iPhone before attempting to sign in again.
cq_2: After you updated your birthday on account.microsoft.com, did you receive any prompts or instructions to verify your age (e.g., by providing a credit card or ID), or did the change seem to take effect immediately without further steps?
solution_2: The persistent parental consent request on iOS, despite your birthday update and desktop access, strongly suggests an incomplete age verification on your Microsoft account. You need to explicitly check your Microsoft profile for any pending age verification steps and complete them, then clear Safari's website data on your iPhone before attempting to sign in again.
cq_3: Could you please visit account.microsoft.com/profile and specifically look for any sections or links related to 'Verify your age' or 'Proof of age' to see if there are any pending actions or status updates required for your birthday change to be fully recognized?
final_solution: Despite updating your birthday, the issue persists due to a likely stuck age verification state. Try this: 1. Go to account.microsoft.com/profile. Temporarily change your birth year to an unambiguously adult year (e.g., 1980), save, then change it back to your correct year and save again. 2. On your iPhone, delete the Skype app, restart your device, then reinstall Skype. 3. Attempt to sign in. This should force a full age re-evaluation and app refresh.
accepted_answer: Hi Tiffany,   We apologize for the inconvenience.   Since Skype is under the umbrella of Microsoft, we have completely migrated and adopted Microsoft’s terms and condition.   With that, you are being asked for a “Parental Permission” is because the system detected that your age on file is under 13 years of age that’s why it was categorized as a “Child Account”.   But you don’t have to worry about this one because we can change the date of birth on your account, so you can have the correct age on file and for us to do it, we strongly recommend several options that you can choose on.   With that, I will be sending you a message and kindly check your email for the details. Thank you!   Marites P.   Skype Community Moderator

accepted_answer:
If a graphic is wrapped, Word automatically creates the caption in a text box; if the graphic is In Line With Text, the caption is ordinary paragraph text. If the graphics in question are not wrapped, then obviously the text boxes are unneeded. In older versions of Word, there was an option to convert the text box to a frame, and you could then select Remove Frame in the Format Frame dialog. In Word 2010, I think you'll just have to cut the text from the text box, paste it into a new paragraph (in the Caption style), and then delete the empty text box. It looks like you've got just five to do, so perhaps that won't be too onerous.

The following macro embeds all Shapes & InlineShapes in a document in tables as InlineShapes with a row for Captions. Whatever positioning applied to the original Shape object will apply to the table also. As coded, the ‘MakeImageTable’ macro limits the inserted InlineShape dimesnsions to 7.5*5.0cm. That code can, of course, be edited/omitted, in which case, so too can the corresponding code that re-sizes the inserted InlineShape to fit.  To have the caption row appear above the image, instead of below, change the Rows(1) references to Rows(2), change the Cell(1, 1) references to Cell(2, 1) and change the change the Cell(2, 1) references to Cell(1, 1).  Note that, due to the extra space taken up by the caption row, all except for the first of the document's pictures are liable to shift position slightly.  Sub AddImageCaptionTables() Dim iShp As InlineShape, Rng As Range, Tbl As Table Dim i As Long, PicWdth As Single, PicHght As Single, VPos As Single Dim HPos As Single, VRel As Long, HRel As Long, BShp As Boolean With ActiveDocument   For i = 1 To .InlineShapes.Count     If .InlineShapes(i).Range.Information(wdWithInTable) = False Then       With .InlineShapes(i)         .LockAspectRatio = True         PicWdth = .Width         PicHght = .Height         Set Rng = .Range       End With       With Rng         If .Characters.Last.Next.Text = vbCr Then .Characters.Last.Next.Text = vbNullString         .InlineShapes(1).Range.Cut       End With       BShp = False: VRel = 0: HRel = 0: VPos = 0: HPos = 0       Call MakeImageTable(Rng, PicWdth, PicHght, BShp, VRel, HRel, VPos, HPos)     End If   Next   While .Shapes.Count > 0     BShp = True     With .Shapes(1)       .LockAspectRatio = True       PicWdth = .Width       PicHght = .Height       VRel = .RelativeVerticalPosition       HRel = .RelativeHorizontalPosition       VPos = .Top       HPos = .Left       Set iShp = .ConvertToInlineShape     End With     With iShp       Set Rng = .Range       .Range.Cut     End With     Call MakeImageTable(Rng, PicWdth, PicHght, BShp, VRel, HRel, VPos, HPos)   Wend End With End Sub  Sub MakeImageTable(Rng As Range, PicWdth As Single, PicHght As Single, BShp As Boolean, _   VRel As Long, HRel As Long, VPos As Single, HPos As Single) Dim Tbl As Table, sngScl As Single, iShp As InlineShape 'Constrain maximum image size If PicWdth > CentimetersToPoints(7.5) Then   sngScl = PicWdth / CentimetersToPoints(7.5)   PicWdth = PicWdth / sngScl   PicHght = PicHght / sngScl End If If PicHght > CentimetersToPoints(5) Then   sngScl = PicHght / CentimetersToPoints(5)   PicWdth = PicWdth / sngScl   PicHght = PicHght / sngScl End If 'Create & format the table Set Tbl = Rng.Tables.Add(Range:=Rng, Numrows:=2, NumColumns:=1) With Tbl   .Borders.Enable = True   .Columns.Width = PicWdth   .TopPadding = 0   .BottomPadding = 0   .LeftPadding = 0   .RightPadding = 0   .Spacing = 0   .Rows(1).HeightRule = wdRowHeightExactly   .Rows(1).Height = PicHght   With .Rows     .LeftIndent = 0     If BShp = True Then       .WrapAroundText = True       .HorizontalPosition = HPos       .RelativeHorizontalPosition = HRel       .VerticalPosition = VPos       .RelativeVerticalPosition = VRel       .AllowOverlap = False     End If   End With   With .Cell(1, 1).Range     With .ParagraphFormat       .SpaceBefore = 0       .SpaceAfter = 0       .LeftIndent = 0       .RightIndent = 0       .FirstLineIndent = 0       .KeepWithNext = True     End With     .Paste     ' Resize the inserted InlineShape     Set iShp = .InlineShapes(1)     With iShp       .Width = PicWdth       .Height = PicHght     End With   End With   With .Cell(2, 1).Range     .Style = "Caption"     .End = .End - 1     .InsertAfter vbCr     .InsertCaption Label:="Figure", TitleAutoText:=" ", Title:="", _       Position:=wdCaptionPositionBelow, ExcludeLabel:=0     .Paragraphs.First.Range.Characters.Last.Text = vbNullString     .Borders(wdBorderLeft).LineStyle = wdLineStyleNone     .Borders(wdBorderRight).LineStyle = wdLineStyleNone     .Borders(wdBorderTop).LineStyle = wdLineStyleNone   End With End With End Sub  To work with just the selected image (or multiple images in a selected range), change: With ActiveDocument to: With Selection and change:   While .Shapes.Count > 0     With .Shapes(1) to:   While .ShapeRange.Count > 0     With .ShapeRange(1)

title: Formula stopped working
original_question: I have a spreadsheet that I have been using for ten months, I simply place the data in the correct columns, and the COUNTIF formulas calculate how many people of a certain profession and pay grade exists. This has worked for ten months, today I go to execute the same thing I have always done and it will not work. Interestingly enough, it work for the first 80% of the data in the worksheet...my mind is blown!    I have attempted to repaste in to the various spreadsheets I've saved over the last ten months, I've tried erasing all of the data and re-inputting, I've checked to make sure the Automatic calculation is on, I have ensured I don't have a circular formula, and I've tried changing the formatting from General to Text...nothing has worked.
preliminary_solution: Verify that the data in the last 20% of your spreadsheet is truly identical in format and content (e.g., no leading/trailing spaces, correct data type) to the data in the working section, as subtle differences can prevent COUNTIF from matching.
cq_1: Could you provide the exact COUNTIF formula you are using and an example of the data in the columns it references, specifically from the section where it stops working?
user_response_1: The formula is =COUNTIFS(Battalion!$D:$D,"CPT",Battalion!$I:$I,"25A"), but I cannot share the data because it contains confidential information.
solution_1: The issue is almost certainly due to subtle, invisible differences in the data within columns D and I in the last 20% of your sheet. Use helper columns with formulas like =TRIM(D2) and =LEN(D2) to identify extra spaces or unexpected character counts in the problematic rows, and consider using =CLEAN(D2) to remove non-printable characters.
final_solution: The issue is almost certainly due to subtle, invisible differences in the data within columns D and I in the last 20% of your sheet. Use helper columns with formulas like =TRIM(D2) and =LEN(D2) to identify extra spaces or unexpected character counts in the problematic rows, and consider using =CLEAN(D2) to remove non-printable characters.
accepted_answer: Check the data for invisible leading or trailing blanks.  You could use the Len() function to compare what you see with what the cell contains. Or you could use   =SEARCH(" ",A1)  and copy down. If it returns a number, there is a blank in the cell. Also, if the data is copied from a web site, it may contain the non-breaking space, which is different from a regular space. To check for these, use  =SEARCH(CHAR(160),A1)
